etymology.description: 人(人) (rén, "man") + 戈(戈) (gē, "spear") = a man with a spear -> to guard against
etymology.interpretation: The character 戍(shù, "to guard against") represents the idea of a person carrying a weapon, symbolizing the act of guarding or defending. The combination of 人(人) (rén, "man") and 戈(戈) (gē, "spear") evokes the image of a soldier or guard standing ready.

examples.chinese: 为了保护家园(為了保護家園)，他在边境戍守(他在邊境戍守)。
examples.pinyin: Wèile bǎohù jiāyuán, tā zài biānjìng shùshǒu.
examples.english: To protect the homeland, he guards at the border.

examples.chinese: 将士们在长城上戍守(將士們在長城上戍守)，以防敌人入侵(以防敵人入侵)。
examples.pinyin: Jiàngshìmen zài Chángchéng shàng shùshǒu, yǐ fáng dírén rùqīn.
examples.english: The soldiers guard on the Great Wall to prevent enemy invasions.

examples.chinese: 日日夜夜地戍守(日日夜夜地戍守)，卫兵从不松懈(衛兵從不鬆懈)。
examples.pinyin: Rìrìyèyè de shùshǒu, wèibīng cóng bù sōngxiè.
examples.english: Guarding day and night, the guards never relent.
